cooperates.cooperate_desc:
🔥 How Karrie & Johnson Work Together

· A Mobile "Bomb": Johnson's ultimate allows him to transform into a fast-moving car that can pick up one ally. With Karrie inside, he effectively becomes a precision-guided weapon that delivers the Marksman directly into the heart of the enemy team. This overcomes Karrie's usual weakness of needing to slowly walk into position or being caught out while rotating.
· Perfect Timing for Maximum Damage: The knock-up and stun from Johnson's crash creates a perfect window for Karrie. She can immediately activate her ultimate (Speedy Lightwheel) and begin shredding key targets. Enemy heroes who are stunned cannot fight back or escape her rapid-fire basic attacks and true damage.
· Protection During Delivery: While riding in Johnson, Karrie is untargetable and immune to crowd control. This safely transports her past dangerous frontlines and zoning skills, allowing her to attack the vulnerable backline (like enemy Mages or Marksmen) from an unexpected angle.

counters.counter_desc:
Hayabusa's strengths directly target Karrie's core weaknesses:

· Exploits Low Durability: Karrie has low HP and defense, making her extremely vulnerable to Hayabusa's high single-target burst damage, which can eliminate her before she can react.
· Negates Her Defense: Karrie's main survival tool is her Phantom Step (Skill 2) dash. Hayabusa's Quad Shadow (Skill 2) gives him superior mobility to chase and stick to her, often negating her escape attempts.
· Perfect Backline Access: As an assassin, Hayabusa specializes in diving past the frontline. He can easily reach Karrie, who must stay at the back to deal damage, and burst her down.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Karrie's "dominant phase" is late game, where her complete "Trinity" build (Corrosion Scythe, Demon Hunter Sword, Golden Staff) allows her to shred any hero, especially tanks, with high attack speed and true damage.

Her strategy changes dramatically throughout the match. Here's how to play her, build you are using, with specific tips for each phase:

🎯 Early Game / Laning Phase

Build Focus: Farming for your first core items, starting with Warrior Boots for defense.
Playstyle Goal:Safe farming, limited aggression.

Laning Precautions & Strategies:

· Start with Skill 1 (Spinning Lightwheel) to clear the first minion wave quickly and pressure the enemy.
· Position the Skill 1 carefully in the center of the minion wave to hit all of them.
· Your main priority is farming safely. Use Skill 1 to last-hit minions from a distance.
· Your only escape is Skill 2 (Phantom Step). Save it to dodge key enemy skills or dash away from ganks. Do not use it aggressively unless you are sure you are safe.
· A simple combo for lane trading is: Skill 1 (to slow) > Skill 2 (to dash in) > basic attacks to stack your passive.

⚡ Mid Game / Rotating Phase

Build Focus: Completing your core "Trinity" damage items (Corrosion Scythe, Demon Hunter Sword, Golden Staff).
Playstyle Goal:Joining skirmishes and objectives, becoming a major damage threat.

Team Fight Precautions & Strategies:

· Always activate your Ultimate (Speedy Lightwheel) before a fight starts for the movement speed and dual lightwheels.
· Use Skill 2 (Phantom Step) primarily for repositioning in fights, not just for damage. Dash sideways to avoid crowd control skills.
· You are a tank's worst nightmare. Focus your attacks on the closest frontline enemy (tank or fighter). Your true damage will melt them.
· Never walk in front of your team. Always position yourself behind your tanks and supports.

🏆 Late Game / Team Fight Phase

Build Focus: Finalizing your build with Wind of Nature and Malefic Roar for survival and armor pen

emblems.emblem_desc: Choosing the Fighter emblem over the typical Marksman emblem trades some late-game physical attack and critical stats for durability and spell vamp. This emblem provides base attributes suited for extended fights, making it a good foundation for a tank-buster playstyle.

equips.equip_desc: This is a classic "Trinity" or attack speed build for Karrie, designed to maximize her true damage potential by rapidly stacking her passive. The strategy revolves around high attack speed and special "attack effects" that are massively amplified by Golden Staff.
equips.equip_title: This is a classic "Trinity" or attack speed

hero.hero_overview:
Karrie, the Lost Star, is a Gold Lane Marksman known for her exceptional ability to defeat tanky heroes. Her damage scales significantly into the late game.

· Role & Specialty: Marksman specializing in Damage/Finisher.
· Core Mechanic: Her power comes from her passive, Lightwheel Mark. Basic attacks and skills apply marks; at 5 stacks, they explode for True Damage based on the target's Max HP. This makes her a premier "tank-buster."
hero.hero_strength:
Karrie's strengths make her a formidable carry against specific team compositions.

Dominates Durable Opponents

· Her passive deals percentage-based True Damage, which ignores defense. She excels at defeating tanks and fighters with high HP and armor.
· Item synergies (like Demon Hunter Sword) further enhance this anti-tank damage.

High Burst & Sustained Damage

· Her ultimate allows her to unleash a rapid barrage of basic attacks, triggering her passive explosion frequently.
· This creates a powerful burst damage window that can swiftly eliminate key targets.

Effective Kiting & Chase

· Phantom Step (Skill 2) is a low-cooldown dash, providing crucial mobility.
· Combined with the slow from Spinning Lightwheel (Skill 1), she can effectively chase fleeing enemies or create distance from threats.
hero.hero_weakness:
To defeat Karrie, you must exploit her inherent vulnerabilities.

Highly Vulnerable to Burst & Crowd Control

· She has low durability ("squishy") and a slow base movement speed.
· She is easily ambushed and defeated by mobile assassins (e.g., Hayabusa, Natalia, Karina) who can bypass the front line.
· Crowd Control (stuns, immobilizes) is her greatest fear, as it prevents her from dodging and dealing damage.

spell.spell_desc:
· Prioritize Safety: It is often better to save Flicker for escape rather than using it aggressively. A living Karrie can deal massive damage.
· Combine with Skill 2: Use Phantom Step to dodge an enemy skill, and use Flicker if you are still in danger. This sequence maximizes your escape distance.
· Communicate with Your Team: Inform your team when your Flicker is on cooldown so they know you are more vulnerable.

hero.hero_overview:

When you use her, it's better for you to farm till level 4 without engaging in any fights before that because it's her ult that's so crucial in any fights but in late her basic attack is enough...and with the new meta uprising in exp she is best to counter them.
